test_results.note: 1 failure is pre-existing flaky test (test_master_class_not_dumpmaster) — race condition with async tasks from prior integration test; passes in isolation. Not related to this feature.

criteria.description: disable_location_services() reads and stores original registry value before writing Deny
criteria.evidence: system_config.py:639 — QueryValueEx(key, _LOCATION_VALUE_NAME) stores result in `original` before SetValueEx(..., 'Deny') at line 643. Returns original on success.

criteria.description: restore_location_services() writes back exact original value; when None → deletes the key; validates value in {'Allow', 'Deny', None}
criteria.evidence: system_config.py:666 — validates original against _LOCATION_VALID_VALUES = frozenset({'Allow', 'Deny'}); None falls through to DeleteValue at line 682; non-None restores via SetValueEx at line 687; invalid values fall back to 'Deny' with warning.

criteria.description: Non-Windows platforms: Location Services functions are no-ops
criteria.evidence: disable_location_services() (line 626): `if sys.platform != 'win32': return None`. restore_location_services() (line 661): `if sys.platform != 'win32': return`. Both return immediately on non-Windows.

criteria.description: New cleanup label 'Location Services restore' added to _VALID_CLEANUP_LABELS and _execute_cleanup_by_label()
criteria.evidence: system_config.py:45 — CLEANUP_LABEL_LOCATION_SERVICES = 'Location Services restore'. _VALID_CLEANUP_LABELS (line 695–702) includes CLEANUP_LABEL_LOCATION_SERVICES. _execute_cleanup_by_label() (line 740–741) handles it with `restore_location_services(original=None)`.

criteria.description: response() injects geolocation-only JS on non-target domains (not full payload)
criteria.evidence: proxy_addon.py:286–295 — `if is_target` injects full `self._js_payload`; else branch (non-target) injects `self._geo_only_payload` built by `_build_geo_only_payload()` which contains only geolocation + permissions overrides, not timezone/language/WebRTC.

criteria.description: request() intercepts exact host==www.googleapis.com + path==/geolocation/v1/geolocate + POST and returns fake JSON with randomized accuracy (40–80m)
criteria.evidence: proxy_addon.py:234–248 — condition checks `flow.request.host == 'www.googleapis.com' and flow.request.path == '/geolocation/v1/geolocate' and flow.request.method == 'POST'`. Returns JSON with `accuracy = random.randint(40, 80)` and fake lat/lon from preset.

criteria.description: navigator.permissions.query override scoped to name === 'geolocation' only
criteria.evidence: inject.js:202 — `if (permissionDesc && permissionDesc.name === 'geolocation')` returns fake granted; else falls through to `return origPermissionsQuery(permissionDesc)`. Also confirmed in _build_geo_only_payload() at proxy_addon.py:110.

criteria.description: Non-target domains with script-src 'none' CSP → injection skipped
criteria.evidence: proxy_addon.py:288–292 — in the non-target branch: `csp = flow.response.headers.get('content-security-policy', '')` then `if csp and _has_restrictive_csp(csp): return`. _has_restrictive_csp() (line 118–135) correctly detects `script-src 'none'` as sole source by parsing directives and checking `tokens[1:] == ["'none'"]`.

criteria.description: request() error handling: try/except around intercept, log on failure, pass through
criteria.evidence: proxy_addon.py:237–249 — `try:` wraps the intercept block; `except Exception as e: logger.error('Geolocation API intercept failed: %s', e)` on line 247–248. Code falls through to the normal Accept-Language rewrite at line 250 without re-raising.

criteria.description: ProxyState gains original_location_services field with backward-compatible default
criteria.evidence: system_config.py:92 — `original_location_services: Optional[str] = None` is declared as a dataclass field with default None, ensuring backward compatibility when deserializing state files that predate this field.
316 tests passed. 1 failure (test_master_class_not_dumpmaster) is a pre-existing flaky race condition unrelated to this feature. 10 skipped tests are pre-existing.
Included in the 316 passing tests. No new test failures introduced by this feature.

summary: All 12 acceptance criteria verified against source code. Implementation is complete and correct: Location Services registry handling is safe (read-before-write, delete on None, validation, non-Windows no-op), cleanup pipeline is fully integrated, JS injection correctly branches on target vs non-target domains with restrictive CSP guard, googleapis.com geolocation intercept matches exact conditions and randomizes accuracy in 40–80m range, permissions query override is scoped to geolocation only, and ProxyState field has backward-compatible default. Test suite shows 316 passing with only one pre-existing flaky failure unrelated to this feature. Feature is ready for deployment.
